questionHtml:
Outline how alloys conduct electricity and why they are often harder than pure metals.

 

Conduct electricity:

Harder than pure metals:
markschemeHtml:
Conduct electricity:
«delocalized/valence» electrons free to move «under potential difference»  [✔]

Harder than pure metals:
atoms/ions of different sizes prevent layers «of atoms/ions» from sliding over one another  [✔]

breadcrumbLabels: Structure 2. Models of bonding and structure
Structure 2.4.3—Alloys are mixtures of a metal and other metals or non-metals. They have enhanced properties. Explain the properties of alloys in terms of non-directional bonding.
